subject: English
title: For Anne Gregory – Medium Short Notes

key_points: The poem 'For Anne Gregory' is written by W. B. Yeats
The poem is structured as a dialogue between a young man and Anne Gregory
The young man believes that Anne Gregory’s beauty, especially her yellow hair, attracts young men
He argues that people cannot love her purely for her inner personality
Anne Gregory challenges this belief by saying she can dye her hair different colours
She suggests that by changing her appearance, someone might love her for her true self
The poem highlights how society often judges people by outward appearance
It questions whether true love can exist beyond physical beauty
An old religious man introduces the idea that only God can love someone for themselves alone
The poem ends by contrasting human love with divine love
characters: Anne Gregory – Represents the desire to be valued for inner personality rather than physical beauty
The Young Man – Symbolises society’s belief that beauty influences attraction
The Religious Man – Represents spiritual wisdom and introduces the concept of divine love
themes: Society’s tendency to judge people based on looks
literary_devices: Metaphor – 'Honey-coloured ramparts' metaphorically describes Anne Gregory’s yellow hair
Symbolism – Her hair symbolises outward beauty that attracts admiration
Dialogue – The poem is written as a conversation between two speakers
Contrast – The poem contrasts physical beauty with inner identity
Philosophical Reflection – The poem raises questions about the nature of love
exam_tips: Mention the dialogue structure of the poem
Discuss Anne Gregory’s idea of changing her hair colour
Highlight the difference between human love and divine love
Focus on the theme of beauty versus inner character
